title: Global Leadership Opportunity: Transatlantic Future Leaders Forum Internship 2026
summary: The Transatlantic Future Leaders Forum (TFLF) is a prestigious internship platform designed to give the brightest Polish university students an opportunity to gain hands-on experience in the offices of top lawmakers in Washington, D.C. and London . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Through this program, participants are immersed in transatlantic policymaking environments, working alongside decision-makers and gaining valuable insights into legislative processes in both the United States and the United Kingdom. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Beyond the internship experience, TFLF provides a robust support and mentoring structure for talented students, helping them build professional skills, expand their networks, and prepare for leadership roles in public life. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); What TFLF Offers TFLF is more than an internship it is a comprehensive leadership development platform that equips participants with knowledge, exposure, and connections. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants benefit from: Internships with leading policymakers in the U.S. Congress and the UK House of Commons Mentorship and career support from experienced professionals Exclusive meetings with leaders in politics, academia, and business Exposure to think tanks, companies, and professional service organizations in both countries ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); A strong network of high-achieving Polish peers and alumni The program is built and led by former interns of U.S. Congress and the UK Parliament who are committed to creating opportunities for the next generation of Polish leaders. Why Apply to TFLF? The Transatlantic Future Leaders Forum offers a rare opportunity to: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Gain real-world experience in global policymaking Learn directly from influential lawmakers and institutions Build a competitive international profile Join a prestigious network of future Polish leaders For ambitious Polish students passionate about public policy, international relations, and leadership, TFLF is a transformative stepping stone toward a global career. Application Process 1. title: Hermès Internships 2026 in Paris & Île-de-France: Paid 6-Month Luxury Industry Opportunities in Communication, Marketing, Finance, Quality, Retail & Product Development
summary: Are you looking to launch your career inside one of the world s most prestigious luxury maisons? Herm s, the iconic French house founded in 1837, is offering multiple paid, full-time internships for 2026 across its Communication, Marketing, Finance, Quality, Product Development, and Retail Education departments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); These six-month internships, primarily based in Paris, Pantin, Fontenay-sous-Bois, and surrounding le-de-France locations, provide exceptional hands-on experience within a global luxury group employing more than 25,000 people worldwide. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Herm s is recognized for its craftsmanship, creativity, entrepreneurial spirit, and commitment to excellence. Interns will work alongside experienced professionals in a dynamic, high-performance environment that values ethics, diversity, inclusion, and responsible management. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); All internships are full-time and paid, lasting six months. Start dates vary between July and September 2026 depending on the position. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); About Herm s: A Global Leader in Luxury Craftsmanship Herm s is a French, family-owned, and independent company with nearly two centuries of heritage. The House operates across sixteen artisanal m tiers, producing leather goods, fashion accessories, crystal, jewelry, saddlery, ready-to-wear, and more. With nearly 300 stores worldwide, Herm s maintains a unique artisanal model rooted in local production in France, respecting both people and nature. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); These internships are hosted within Herm s International (the holding company), Herm s Sellier, Herm s Manufacture de M taux, and Cristallerie Saint Louis. title: Apple Finance Internship 2026: Launch Your Career with Apple’s Finance Development Program
summary: Global technology leader Apple Inc. is now offering multiple Finance Internship opportunities for students passionate about building careers in finance, analytics, and business strategy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); This highly competitive internship provides hands-on experience within Apple s Finance organization and a potential pathway into the prestigious Finance Development Program (FDP). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); If you are targeting a career in corporate finance, financial analysis, or data-driven business strategy, this is an opportunity to work at one of the most influential companies in the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Apple Finance Internship Apple s Finance Internship allows students to work alongside top finance professionals while supporting key areas of the business. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Interns may be placed within departments such as: AppleCare Financial Services Internet Software Services Marketing Operations Research Development Retail Sales Worldwide Financial Planning Analysis And more During the internship, participants contribute directly to real business projects that impact Apple s global operations. What You ll Do as a Finance Intern As an intern, you will gain exposure to advanced financial and analytical work, including: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Conducting in-depth financial analysis using advanced Excel functions Working with SQL and data tools Creating dashboards using data visualization platforms such as Tableau Designing and optimizing data-driven reporting systems Building automated financial models and reporting solutions Collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver strategic insights Identifying operational efficiencies through analytics and technical platforms This internship is structured to help students develop technical, analytical, and business communication skills at a world-class level. Pathway to the Finance Development Program (FDP) Successful summer interns may become eligible to join Apple s Finance Development Program (FDP) . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The FDP is a 12 18 month rotational program designed for entry-level finance talent. Participants typically rotate across four to six finance functions, gaining broad exposure across Apple s business. Upon completion of the FDP, participants transition into permanent Financial Analyst roles within Apple. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); This internship is therefore not just a summer role it can serve as a launchpad for a long-term finance career at Apple. Minimum Qualifications Applicants must: Be pursuing a Bachelor s degree in: Finance Data Science Mathematics Statistics Business Administration Economics Accounting Or a related field Have an expected graduation date of Winter 2027 or Spring 2028 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Application materials must include: Resume Cover letter Unofficial transcript Applications without these documents will not be considered. Preferred Qualifications Strong candidates will demonstrate: Advanced Excel proficiency Experience with SQL or Python (preferred but not required) Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Ability to thrive in fast-paced and ambiguous environments Excellent written and verbal communication skills Curiosity, initiative, and accountability Why You Should Apply This internship offers: Exposure to Apple s global finance operations Hands-on experience with advanced financial analytics Cross-functional collaboration experience Mentorship from leading finance professionals A potential pathway into Apple s Finance Development Program Career acceleration within a globally recognized technology company Apple is committed to inclusion and diversity and provides equal opportunity to all qualified applicants. title: UNEP Traditional Knowledge Internship 2026 (Remote Option Available): Work on Global Biodiversity Policy Ahead of COP17
summary: The Secretariat of the Convention on Biological Diversity under the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) is now accepting applications for a six-month internship focused on traditional knowledge and engagement with indigenous peoples and local communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); This opportunity is linked to preparations for major Convention on Biological Diversity processes, including COP17 and the Nature and Culture Summit. The internship can be undertaken at the Montreal duty station in Canada or remotely. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The application deadline is March 7, 2026. About the UNEP Internship 2026 Hosted by the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP), this internship supports work under the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD). The selected intern will contribute to biodiversity governance efforts that integrate culture, indigenous knowledge systems, and environmental sustainability. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The internship provides emerging professionals with exposure to multilateral environmental negotiations and global biodiversity policymaking in preparation for COP17. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Internship Location Montreal, Canada (Duty Station) Remote option available This flexibility allows eligible candidates worldwide to participate without relocation, depending on arrangements. Internship Focus and Responsibilities The intern will support research, drafting, and coordination activities related to biodiversity, culture, and traditional knowledge systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Key responsibilities include: Conducting research on biodiversity, indigenous peoples, and traditional knowledge Drafting background documents and meeting materials Supporting coordination with stakeholders and indigenous representatives Assisting in preparations for COP17 discussions Contributing to engagement processes linked to the Nature and Culture Summit This role provides hands-on experience in global environmental governance and collaboration with indigenous communities at the international level. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Language Requirements Fluency in English (oral and written) is mandatory Knowledge of French and/or Spanish is considered an asset Financial Information This internship is unpaid. Interns are responsible for: Travel arrangements Visa costs Accommodation Living expenses The United Nations does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet one of the following academic requirements: Be enrolled in or have completed a Master s or PhD programme ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Be in the final year of a Bachelor s degree Have recently completed a Bachelor s degree Candidates should demonstrate: Academic background in environmental studies, indigenous studies, law, public policy, or related disciplines Strong analytical and writing skills Knowledge of indigenous peoples, traditional knowledge, or biodiversity governance Computer literacy in standard software tools Interest in multilateral environmental processes Ability to work across diverse cultural contexts Alignment with the values of the United Nations Professional work experience is not mandatory, but relevant academic specialization is required. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Why You Should Apply This internship is ideal for students and recent graduates interested in: Global biodiversity governance Indigenous knowledge systems Environmental law and policy Multilateral negotiations International environmental diplomacy It offers rare exposure to international policy processes leading up to COP17, making it a valuable stepping stone for careers in environmental governance and global development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); How to Apply Interested applicants should scroll to the official link provided by the host institution and submit their application before the deadline. The final date to apply is March 7, 2026. Early submission is strongly recommended.
